title: ceropegia mammillaris
intro: Ceropegia mammillaris, also known as the 'fishbone cactus', is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Ceropegia mammillaris include relieving pain, reducing fever, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions or decoctions, and it can also be applied topically as a poultice to aid in wound healing.
